title: TeraWulf's AI Compute Revenue Outpaces Bitcoin Mining Amid $427 Million Loss
text: TeraWulf reported a net loss of $427 million in Q1 2026, significantly higher than the $61.4 million loss in Q1 2025. The company generated $34 million in total revenues during the quarter. Artificial intelligence and high-performance computing (HPC) revenues dominated operations, accounting for $21 million—or 60% of total revenues—representing a 117% quarter-over-quarter increase. In stark contrast, Bitcoin mining revenues dropped 50% sequentially to approximately $13 million, reflecting the company's strategic pivot away from cryptocurrency mining. CEO Paul Prager stated the company entered the year with a fully established platform and is now generating operating performance. The firm is leveraging a Google-backed deal with FluidStack, a 25-year arrangement valued at approximately $9.5 billion in contracted revenues, to drive this transition toward more predictable, recurring revenue streams compared to volatile Bitcoin mining returns. The company concluded the quarter with roughly $3.1 billion in cash reserves, providing substantial capital for continued expansion in AI compute services.
